---
name: Taxonomy Contribution Proposal
description: Propose a new technique, category, or structural change to the MLCommons Jailbreak Attack Taxonomy
labels: ["contribution", "triage"]
---

## Contribution Type

Select the type of contribution you are proposing:

- [ ] New Leaf (attack mechanism)
- [ ] New Category (grouping of mechanisms)
- [ ] New Family (top-level branch)
- [ ] Attack Placement (placing an existing attack in the taxonomy)
- [ ] Variant Enrichment (adding variants or evidence to existing mechanism)
- [ ] Structural Improvement (reorganizing existing categories or refining definitions)

## Proposed Placement

Where does this fit in the taxonomy? Provide the family, category, and leaf (if applicable).

**Family > Category > Leaf:**
[e.g., "Perturbation > Instruction Injection > New Mechanism Name"]

Or, if uncertain:

**Current similar mechanisms:**
[List existing taxonomy entries this is related to]

## Brief Description

What is this jailbreak technique or taxonomy refinement? Describe the mechanism structurally: how does it work, and what transformation does it apply to prompts or model context?

## Evidence Summary

For new attack placements: summarize your testing evidence. Minimum 3 open-weight models tested.

| Model | Success Rate | Test Count | Notes |
|-------|--------------|-----------|-------|
| | | | |
| | | | |
| | | | |

For other contribution types, describe what evidence or research supports this addition.

## Relevant References

Cite academic papers, practitioner reports, prior taxonomy versions, or community discussions that support this contribution.

- 
- 
- 

## Additional Context

Include any relevant discussion, related attacks, open questions, or reasoning that helps maintainers evaluate this proposal. Do not include copy-paste attack prompts; describe mechanisms structurally only.

## Checklist

- [ ] I have reviewed the existing taxonomy to verify this is not a duplicate
- [ ] I have not included copy-paste attack prompts in this issue
- [ ] I understand this taxonomy is CC-BY-4.0 licensed and my contribution will be openly available
- [ ] I am prepared to iterate on feedback and potentially submit a formal PR using the submission template
